advantages: O(log n) — 20 comparisons covers a million elements, 30 covers a billion.
Generalizes beyond arrays: any monotonic predicate can be binary-searched.
Python's bisect ships a fast, tested C implementation; no need to reinvent.
disadvantages: Requires sorted input; sorting first costs O(n log n) and you must keep it sorted on updates.
Random access (O(1) by index) is assumed — wrong choice for linked lists.
Off-by-one errors and infinite loops are easy on hand-written versions.
For a one-off lookup on unordered data, a single linear scan or a one-pass set construction is simpler.
